title: UniFormer: Efficient and Unified Model-Centric Scaling for Industrial Recommendation
abstract: arXiv:2606.27058v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recently, substantial progress has been made in industrial recommendation through component-centric model scaling, where individual components such as behavior modeling, feature interaction, or task modeling are independently scaled to improve model capacity. Although recent methods such as HyFormer and OneTrans further explore cross-module co-scaling by jointly modeling behavior and interaction, their designs are still confined to the feature space and lack a unified model-centric scaling framework over the overall modeling space. In this paper, we propose UniFormer, an efficient and unified model-centric scaling framework for industrial recommender systems. To improve efficiency, UniFormer decomposes the overall modeling space into feature and task spaces, which are modeled by stacked Feature-space Interaction Modules and Task-space Interaction Modules, respectively. Moreover, UniFormer introduces semantic-based tokenization scheme to

Abstract: arXiv:2606.27058v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recently, substantial progress has been made in industrial recommendation through component-centric model scaling, where individual components such as behavior modeling, feature interaction, or task modeling are independently scaled to improve model capacity. translated_title: UniFormer：面向工业推荐的高效统一模型中心化扩展方法
translated_abstract: 近期，工业推荐系统通过以组件为中心的模型扩展取得了显著进展，其中行为建模、特征交互或任务建模等独立组件被分别扩展以提升模型容量。尽管HyFormer和OneTrans等最新方法通过联合建模行为与交互探索了跨模块协同扩展，但其设计仍局限于特征空间，缺乏覆盖整体建模空间的统一模型中心化扩展框架。本文提出UniFormer，一种面向工业推荐系统的高效统一模型中心化扩展框架。为提升效率，UniFormer将整体建模空间分解为特征空间和任务空间，分别由堆叠的特征空间交互模块和任务空间交互模块进行建模。此外，UniFormer引入了基于语义的令牌化方案，以增强模型表达能力。
tldr: UniFormer通过将建模空间分解为特征和任务空间，并引入语义化令牌方案，实现了高效统一的工业推荐模型扩展，突破了传统组件独立扩展的局限。
en_tdlr: UniFormer achieves efficient and unified model-centric scaling for industrial recommendations by decomposing the modeling space into feature and task spaces with a semantic-based tokenization scheme, overcoming the limitations of component-centric scaling.
